subject: Cross-Curricular Classroom Sampling
topic: Random class visual sampling across subjects

science8_ecosystem_cer.title: Science 8 CER: Ecosystem Change
science8_ecosystem_cer.subtitle: Use evidence to explain a food-web shift
science8_ecosystem_cer.question: How could removing one species change the rest of an ecosystem?
science8_ecosystem_cer.learning_target: I can explain an ecosystem change using evidence and reasoning.
science8_ecosystem_cer.directions: Choose one organism from the food web. Explain what could change if that organism disappeared or increased suddenly.
sentence_starters: One food-web detail that supports this is...
Another organism that could be affected is...
success_criteria: My claim answers the question.
My evidence uses a food-web detail.
My reasoning explains the connection.
I named more than one affected organism.

math8_ratio_decision.title: Math 8 Unit Rate Decision Matrix
math8_ratio_decision.subtitle: Compare options before choosing the better value
math8_ratio_decision.prompt: Compare three snack-pack options. Use unit rate, total cost, and serving size before deciding which option is the best value.
success_criteria: I calculated or estimated unit rate.
I compared at least two options.
I explained my choice using math vocabulary.

socials9_primary_source_notes.title: Primary Source Two-Column Notes
socials9_primary_source_notes.subtitle: Evidence on one side, interpretation on the other
socials9_primary_source_notes.learning_target: I can separate what a source says from what I infer.
socials9_primary_source_notes.directions: Put source details on the left. Put inferences, questions, or reliability notes on the right.
socials9_primary_source_notes.summary_prompt: What does this source help us understand?

careers8_workplace_scenario.title: Workplace Scenario Planner
careers8_workplace_scenario.subtitle: Choose a response and explain the impact
careers8_workplace_scenario.prompt: Read a short workplace scenario. List possible responses, possible impacts, and the response you would choose.
success_criteria: I considered more than one response.
I explained how people could be affected.
My choice is respectful and realistic.

english8_short_story_response.title: Short Story Inference Response
english8_short_story_response.subtitle: Use a moment from the story to explain character change
english8_short_story_response.learning_target: I can make an inference about a character and support it with evidence.
english8_short_story_response.directions: Choose one moment where the character's thinking or behaviour changes. Use a short evidence note and explain your inference.
english8_short_story_response.prompt: What does this moment reveal about the character?

vocabulary.definition: an idea based on evidence and reasoning
the reason a character acts or reacts
a moment when something important changes

success_criteria: I named a specific moment.
I used an evidence note.
I explained what the moment reveals.
I connected the moment to character change.

adst8_design_brief.title: ADST Design Brief Organizer
adst8_design_brief.subtitle: Problem, users, constraints, and prototype idea
adst8_design_brief.prompt: Plan a simple classroom prototype. Identify the user, the problem, the constraints, and one design choice you will test first.
success_criteria: I named the user and problem.
I listed at least one constraint.
My prototype idea can be tested.

health8_media_balance_exit.title: Media Balance Exit Reflection
health8_media_balance_exit.subtitle: Notice one habit and choose one adjustment
health8_media_balance_exit.learning_target: I can reflect on a media habit and choose a realistic next step.
health8_media_balance_exit.prompt: Which media habit helps you, and which one might need an adjustment?
success_criteria: I named one specific habit.
I explained how it affects me.
I chose one realistic adjustment.

science9_lab_cornell.title: Science Lab Cornell Notes
science9_lab_cornell.subtitle: Observations, patterns, and questions
science9_lab_cornell.learning_target: I can record observations and separate them from inferences.
science9_lab_cornell.directions: Use the cue column for variables, safety notes, and questions. Use the notes column for observations and measurements.
science9_lab_cornell.summary_prompt: What pattern or result matters most?
science9_lab_cornell.reflection_prompt: What should we test or clarify next?

workplace_math10_budget.title: Budget Comparison Organizer
workplace_math10_budget.subtitle: Needs, wants, costs, and trade-offs
workplace_math10_budget.prompt: Compare two monthly budget choices. Consider fixed costs, flexible spending, savings, and one trade-off.
success_criteria: I separated needs from wants.
I included a cost or estimate.
I explained a trade-off.

literacy_word_study_prefix.title: Word Study: Prefix + Root
literacy_word_study_prefix.subtitle: Break the word before guessing the meaning
literacy_word_study_prefix.directions: Choose four words. Circle the prefix, underline the root, and use both parts to predict the meaning.
literacy_word_study_prefix.prompt: Focus prefix: re- / pre- / un-. Explain how the prefix changes the meaning of the root word.
success_criteria: I marked the prefix and root.
I predicted the meaning before checking.
I used one word in a clear sentence.
